title: Nera Dantels
content:

{{Quote|You never know what a smuggler's going to be like. But Captain Dantels was beautiful, Hobbie. Exotic. My type!|Biggs Darklighter|<ref name="Empire12">[[Empire 12|''Empire'' 12]]</ref>}}
'''Nera Dantels''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Starduster]]'' who joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Alliance had a critical shortage of [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]], and Dantels arranged to [[Mission to Commenor (0 BBY)|rendezvous]] with members of [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] on [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]] to deliver a dozen [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2]]- and [[R5-series astromech droid]]s to the Rebels. The group came under attack from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] as they attempted to leave the [[Commenor system/Legends|Commenor system]], but Dantels took the ''Starduster'' to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and followed Red Squadron to the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. While there, Dantels helped Red Squadron [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] to find a rare [[Plant/Legends|plant]] in the jungle, an extract from which was used to treat pilot [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]], who was suffering from an unknown infection. Dantels and Darklighter began a brief relationship, but Darklighter was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|combat]] against the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] soon after.

[[0 ABY/Legends|Seven months later]], Dantels was part of a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] team sent to [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]] to negotiate an alliance with the [[Jabiimi Loyalists]]. When the [[Jabiimi]] realized that [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was the son of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], who had abandoned them during the [[Battle of Jabiim|Second Battle of Jabiim]] in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Dantels and the other Rebels were taken into custody. Escaping during a subsequent Imperial [[Subjugation of Jabiim|attack]] on the Loyalist base, Dantels and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] reached the ''Starduster'' and returned to rescue Skywalker. Unable to help the Loyalists as the Imperials began an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], Dantels and the other Rebels were forced to flee. Two [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Jabiim mission, Dantels joined [[Dagger Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Dagger Squadron]] in a [[Attack on Bannistar Station|raid]] to rescue a Rebel team that had been stranded on the Imperial [[Fuel/Legends|refueling]] and supply depot [[Bannistar Station]].

==Biography==
===Rebel smuggler===
{{Quote|Don't worry ''too'' much about me! In this business, a girl learns to ''punch'' a few "eyes" when she needs to!|Nera Dantels|<ref name="XW0.5" />}}
The early life of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Nera Dantels was shrouded in mystery, but by the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] she was an experienced [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]<ref name="FF126" /> and [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Starduster]]'',<ref name="XW0.5" /> with contacts on most of the major [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] and a reputation for being able to obtain almost anything. Around this [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] was in desperate need of weapons and [[Starship/Legends|ships]] for their fight against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Choosing to abandon her neutrality, Dantels decided to assist the Rebellion by helping to acquire the necessary supplies.<ref name="FF126" />

[[File:Dantels-Starduster.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Nera Dantels, outside the ''Starduster'']]
Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="XW0.5" /> in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the Alliance acquired a number of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] from an [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] assembly facility, but a critical shortage of the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]] used for [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Astrogation/Legends|navigation]] and systems management left them unable to use the new [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Aware of the Alliance's supply problems, the Galactic Empire placed strict controls on astromech [[Droid/Legends|droids]] in order to keep them out of Rebel hands.<ref name="Empire12" /> The Alliance contacted Dantels and recruited her to obtain and deliver a cargo of astromech droids.<ref name="FF126" /> Dantels acquired some droids and attempted to make a delivery to the Rebels, but she was almost caught when [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] broke the Rebel underground on [[Averam/Legends|Averam]] while she was on the planet. Although she got her people to safety, her cargo was lost in the process.<ref name="XW0.5" />

By calling in favors from some of her contacts and bribing others,<ref name="FF126" /> Dantels acquired a dozen more [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2]]- and [[R5-series astromech droid]]s, and once again attempted to smuggle them to the Alliance, arranging to [[Mission to Commenor (0 BBY)|meet]] with Rebel operatives on the Imperial stronghold world of [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]]. A Rebel team consisting of [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]], [[Cesi Eirriss]], and [[Jek Tono Porkins/Legends|Jek Porkins]] proceeded to Commenor,<ref name="XW0.5" /> where they were told to play [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] in a local [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] until Dantels made contact.<ref name="Empire12" /> Shortly after they arrived, Dantels initiated an override tap into the flux field of one of Antilles's cards, changing the image on the card to direct the Rebels to meet her at [[Hangar Bay 31]], where she was waiting with the ''Starduster''. After meeting with the Rebels, Dantels agreed to meet them in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] and follow them back to their destination. Upon leaving the planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], however, Dantels detected [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] approaching from a secret Imperial base on Commenor's [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Folor]].<ref name="XW0.5" />

Antilles and Darklighter moved to intercept the oncoming [[TIE series/Legends|TIEs]] in their X-wings, leaving Eirriss and Porkins to defend the ''Starduster''. With help from Dantels, using the [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] aboard her vessel to destroy two Imperial fighters that made it past her escort, Red Squadron soon eliminated the first wave. However, Eirriss's fighter was damaged in the [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]]. Antilles instructed Eirriss to eject so that Dantels could pick her up in the ''Starduster'', but Eirriss instead set a collision course for the oncoming second wave of Imperial fighters, telling the other Rebels to leave without her. Dantels and the others jumped to hyperspace moments after Eirriss's starfighter collided with the Imperials, and proceeded to the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="XW0.5" />

===The Battle of Yavin===
{{Quote|Biggs Darklighter, listen to me… Come back, do you hear? Promise me.|Nera Dantels, to Biggs Darklighter|<ref name="Empire15">[[Empire 15|''Empire'' 15]]</ref>}}
[[File:Dantels-Darklighter.jpg|thumb|right|140px|Nera Dantels and Biggs Darklighter]]
After personally delivering the astromech droids to [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]],<ref name="FF126" /> the head of the Alliance's [[Starfighter Command (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Starfighter Command]],<ref name="XW:OSG">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> Dantels remained on Yavin 4 and agreed to go on a date with Darklighter, spending the evening walking the parapets of the ancient [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] Temple that served as the Alliance base. Sensing that Darklighter was distracted, Dantels asked what was wrong, and he explained that his friend and fellow pilot [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]] was [[Death/Legends|dying]]<ref name="FF126" /> from an unknown disease that was resistant to [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] therapy.<ref name="Empire15" /> A few days later, she offered to accompany Darklighter and [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Shae Polikex]] into the Yavin 4 jungle<ref name="FF126" /> in search of a [[Plant/Legends|plant]] extract that had cured members of the original [[Scout/Legends|survey]] team who had fallen victim to a similar infection.<ref name="Empire15" />

As the three Rebels proceeded through the jungle, Darklighter was attacked by a swarm of [[viper wasp]]s, but Dantels repelled the insects with a [[Smoke grenade/Legends|smoke grenade]]. Soon after, a large [[mamien]] attacked the group, taking Darklighter by surprise and knocking him to the ground. As it moved in for the kill, Dantels opened fire with her [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. The creature survived her attack, but as it turned its attention on her, Darklighter reached his [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] and used it to kill the predator. Pressing on once more, the group eventually came across the plant they needed and retrieved an extract. They were still in the jungle when Darklighter received a call on his [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] ordering him back to base—the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] had entered the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] and was moving to destroy the Rebel base.<ref name="Empire15" />

After returning to base, Darklighter prepared to participate in a Rebel attack on the approaching battlestation.<ref name="Empire15" /> Dantels promised that, if anything happened to him, she would take care of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], his old friend from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] who had recently joined the Alliance.<ref name="Rebellion13">[[Rebellion 13|''Rebellion'' 13]]</ref> Before he left, she approached Darklighter in the base's [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and told him to come back safely, kissing him and leaving without waiting for an answer.<ref name="Empire15" /> The Rebels suffered heavy losses in the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], and Darklighter was among those killed before Skywalker destroyed the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]].<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> Following the battle, Dantels visited a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] memorial to Darklighter with Klivian, who had recovered from his infection after treatment with the plant extract.<ref name="Empire15" />

===Diplomacy on Jabiim===
{{Quote|You should know better than to sneak up on a girl with a blaster.|Nera Dantels|<ref name="Empire33">[[Empire 33|''Empire'' 33]]</ref>}}
[[File:NeraLeia.jpg|thumb|left|210px|Dantels on Jabiim with Leia Organa]]
Dantels continued to work with the Alliance over the following [[Standard month/Legends|months]], putting her piloting skills at their disposal<ref name="RECG" /> and earning the respect of her fellow pilots, including [[Vin Nothos]], whom she saved when they were ambushed on a supply run through the [[Momansi Belt]].<ref name="Rebellion13" /> [[0 ABY/Legends|Seven months after]] the Battle of Yavin, the [[Jabiimi Loyalists|Jabiimi Loyalist]] leader [[Nolan Gillmunn]] invited Alliance representatives to the war-torn planet [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]] to request their assistance in his fight against Imperial occupation and the [[Jabiimi Nationalists|Jabiimi Nationalist party]]. Dantels was selected to join [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], statistician [[Jorin Sol]], and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] pilots Luke Skywalker, Wedge Antilles, and Darek Klivian in the [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] party. With Dantels carrying Organa and Sol aboard the ''Starduster'', the Rebels arrived at Jabiim to find Gillmunn's Loyalists under attack by [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] and Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Despite eliminating the attacking Imperials, Dantels and the other Rebels were greeted with suspicion by the [[Jabiimi]], who distrusted off-worlders since they had been abandoned by the forces of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] during the [[Battle of Jabiim]] in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. However, Gillmunn welcomed the group, and, while Antilles and Klivian kept patrol in orbit, Dantels and the other Rebels went with Gillmunn to the Loyalist base.<ref name="Empire29">[[Empire 29|''Empire'' 29]]</ref>

[[File:Dantels-Organa.jpg|thumb|right|140px|Dantels and Leia Organa]]
The negotiations did not last long, however; when the Jabiimi realized that Luke Skywalker was the son of the man who had betrayed them [[21 BBY/Legends|twenty years earlier]], they attempted to attack the Rebel party. Although Gillmunn prevented the situation from turning to violence,<ref name="Empire29" /> Dantels, Organa, and Sol were taken into custody and separated from Skywalker. Locked up and placed under guard, Dantels and Organa discussed potential escape plans but decided to wait and see how the situation developed.<ref name="Empire30">[[Empire 30|''Empire'' 30]]</ref> Before long, the Loyalist base came under [[Subjugation of Jabiim|attack]] by Imperial and Jabiimi Nationalist forces. Deciding that they could wait no longer, the Rebels fled their [[Prison/Legends|cell]], accompanied by their guard, [[Luscen]]. As they made their way out of the base, they were attacked by a pair of stormtroopers, who shot Luscen and ordered the others to surrender. Moving quickly, Dantels grabbed the fallen guard's [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] pistol and shot both stormtroopers dead. At that moment, the Rebels were approached by the Jabiimi Loyalist [[Tal Hesz]], who told them that Skywalker was with Gillmunn and that they were to rendezvous in the outlying hills.<ref name="Empire32">[[Empire 32|''Empire'' 32]]</ref>

Following Hesz to a pair of [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]], the Rebels set off for the rendezvous point, with Dantels and Organa following on one bike, while Hesz and Sol took the lead on the other. Before long, Hesz stopped at the edge of a [[Mine/Legends|minefield]] running through a narrow canyon. As the Imperials approached from behind, Organa decided to press on, hoping that her speeder bike could outrun the exploding mines. Hesz, however, had sold out the Rebels and allowed both himself and Sol to be captured by the Imperials. With their speeder damaged by the mines, the two remaining Rebels were unable to help Sol and pressed on to the ''Starduster''.<ref name="Empire32" /> They arrived to find Organa's droids [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] pinned down near the ship by an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]] and several stormtroopers. Using the droids as a distraction, Dantels and Organa sneaked aboard the ''Starduster'', and Dantels used its [[Laser/Legends|laser]] cannons to eliminate the Imperials.<ref name="Empire33" />

After recovering the droids, the pair were joined by Antilles and Klivian, who had broken through an [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial armada]] that had arrived in orbit. Organa hoped to save the Jabiimi, but Dantels and Antilles persuaded her that there was nothing they could do; their only hope was to escape and send reinforcements.<ref name="Empire33" /> As the Imperial forces began an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the planet, Dantels and the other Rebels headed back to the Loyalist base in search of Skywalker and Gillmunn. After Antilles and Klivian cleared the area of Imperials, Dantels took the ''Starduster'' down to retrieve their allies. Gillmunn refused to leave but insisted that the Rebels could not save the planet and should flee. As Gillmunn led the surviving Loyalists to the relative safety of the planet's underground [[Mining/Legends|mines]], Skywalker boarded the ''Starduster'', and Dantels took the ship to the safety of hyperspace.<ref name="Empire34">[[Empire 34|''Empire'' 34]]</ref>

===Rescue from Bannistar Station===
{{Quote|I can't believe what I'm hearing! This is Luke and Leia we're talking about. If you're not willing to do everything in your power to ''save'' them, I'll do it ''myself''!|Nera Dantels|<ref name="Rebellion12">[[Rebellion 12|''Rebellion'' 12]]</ref>}}
Two months later, the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] came under [[Disaster at Deepspace Besh|attack]] by elements of the Imperial Navy commanded by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader and was forced to flee into hyperspace.<ref name="Rebellion5">[[Rebellion 5|''Rebellion'' 5]]</ref> The [[Providence-class carrier/destroyer/Legends|''Providence''-class carrier/destroyer]] ''[[Rebel One]]'' failed to rendezvous with the rest of the [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]], and Dantels was among several [[Officer/Legends|officers]] on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], when a message was received from Alliance [[Spy/Legends|spymaster]] [[Tungo Li]] aboard ''Rebel One'' advising them that the ship had become stranded at [[Bannistar Station]],<ref name="Rebellion12" /> a major Imperial [[Fuel/Legends|refueling]] and supply hub in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="Rebellion11">[[Rebellion 11|''Rebellion'' 11]]</ref> Li informed the Rebels that ''Rebel One''{{'s}} crew, including Organa and Skywalker, was planning to [[Attack on Bannistar Station|infiltrate and destroy]] the [[Space station/Legends|station]] and that they did not expect to survive.<ref name="Rebellion12" />

[[File:DantelsB-Wing.jpg|thumb|left|210px|Dantels flies a B-wing during the attack on Bannistar Station.]]
When Ackbar refused to risk elements of the Fleet to rescue them, insisting that they were unprepared to charge into enemy territory, Dantels and [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Netrem Pollard/Legends|Netrem Pollard]] of [[Dagger Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Dagger Squadron]] resolved to come up with another option.<ref name="Rebellion12" /> The Rebels soon intercepted a transmission revealing that Organa and Skywalker had been captured, and, despite Ackbar's reservations, Pollard planned to take Dagger Squadron's [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighters]] to rescue them. Dantels volunteered to accompany them in the ''Starduster'', but Pollard felt that the ''Starduster'' was not nimble enough and would jeopardize the mission. When Dantels insisted, however, he reluctantly agreed to let her fly one of Dagger Squadron's B-wings, joining Pollard, Vin Nothos, and [[Callen Troyt]] in place of the [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] pilot [[Suskafoo]].<ref name="Rebellion13" />

By the time Dagger Squadron arrived in the [[Bannistar Station system/Legends|system]], Lieutenant [[Deena Shan]], having avoided capture with the other Rebels on Bannistar Station, had destroyed a large section of the station with a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]], providing a distraction that allowed her comrades to escape. Shan rendezvoused with Organa, Skywalker, and the other surviving Rebel operatives, [[Basso]] and [[Able]], and the group attempted to flee in a stolen [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]]. Dagger Squadron intercepted the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] as it lifted from the station and took up escort positions as TIE fighters launched to stop the fleeing Rebels. While Nothos and Troyt cleared a path through the nearby Imperial [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], Dantels, acting as Pollard's [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]], helped to protect the shuttle from the approaching TIEs. When they were clear, all five Rebel ships jumped to hyperspace and returned to ''Home One''.<ref name="Rebellion14">[[Rebellion 14|''Rebellion'' 14]]</ref> Dantels remained a member of Dagger Squadron following their return, though she also continued to fly the ''Starduster''.<ref name="RECG" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You sound like you've been at this awhile.''"<br />"''Oh, only a couple of lifetimes.|Biggs Darklighter and Nera Dantels|<ref name="XW0.5" />}}
[[File:Dantels-tattoo.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Nera Dantels]]
By the time she joined the Rebellion, Nera Dantels was already an experienced smuggler who was known for her ability to acquire almost anything from her network of contacts throughout the galaxy. Her skills as a smuggler made her very useful to the fledgling Rebel Alliance, and she was willing to abandon her professional neutrality to join them.<ref name="FF126" /> Passionate and headstrong, with her individuality proclaimed by the [[Color/Legends|blue]], curved [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]] around her [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], Dantels refused to conform and was not willing to let either rules or people stand in her way.<ref name="RECG" /> She did not want to wait around to be rescued when captured by the Jabiimi,<ref name="Empire30" /> and, when her friends were in danger on Bannistar Station, she refused to obey Admiral Ackbar's order not to attempt to rescue them.<ref name="Rebellion12" /> Although Dantels was accused of taking unnecessary risks,<ref name="Empire32" /> her experience and skill as a pilot earned her the respect of her peers, and, despite Netrem Pollard's initial concerns that her skills as a solo pilot would not translate to flying in a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]],<ref name="Rebellion13" /> she successfully adapted to working as part of Dagger Squadron,<ref name="Rebellion14" /> becoming one of the first [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilots]] to fly the B-wing starfighter in the process. Dantels was also skilled with a blaster pistol.<ref name="RECG" />

Tall and slender, with dark hair, pale skin, and facial tattoos, Dantels had a striking appearance,<ref name="FF126" /> which Biggs Darklighter described as exotic.<ref name="Empire12" /> Darklighter found himself attracted to Dantels when he first saw her on Commenor and, fearing that somebody else would beat him to it, wasted no time in asking her out.<ref name="FF126" /> Dantels agreed to a date walking along the parapets of the Massassi Temple on Yavin 4<ref name="Empire12" /> and soon found herself breaking her professional rules by falling in love with the young pilot.<ref name="FF126" /> Dantels was concerned for Darklighter's safety after he was attacked by a mamien in the jungles of Yavin 4 and before he left to fight in the Battle of Yavin, visiting him in the hangar and bidding him to return to her.<ref name="Empire15" /> When Darklighter was killed in the battle, Dantels was stricken with grief<ref name="FF126" /> and joined Darklighter's friend, Derek Klivian, in visiting his holographic memorial in the Rebel base to pay her respects.<ref name="Empire15" /> After Darklighter's death, Dantels was determined to see through her promise to him that she would take care of Luke Skywalker if anything happened to Darklighter.<ref name="Rebellion13" />

==Behind the scenes==
Nera Dantels was created by author [[Michael A. Stackpole]] for the [[Dark Horse Comics]] special ''[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron ½]]'',<ref name="XW0.5" /> published on [[February 1]], [[1997]].<ref name="DarkHorse">{{DarkHorse|url=Products/96-789/Star-Wars-X-Wing-Rogue-Squadron-1-2-Special-Wizard-Magazine-comic|text=Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron #1/2 (Special Wizard Magazine comic)}}</ref> The events of ''Rogue Squadron ½'' were later retold from Biggs Darklighter's perspective in the [[Empire 12|third issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series' [[Star Wars: Empire: Darklighter|''Darklighter'']] story arc, written by [[Paul Chadwick]] and released in [[2003]].<ref name="XW0.5" /><ref name="Empire12" /> [[Empire 15|''Darklighter, Part 4'']] expanded on Dantels' time on Yavin 4 and her relationship with Darklighter.<ref name="Empire15" /> She later appeared again in ''Empire''{{'s}} [[Star Wars: Empire: In the Shadows of Their Fathers|''In the Shadows of Their Fathers'']] arc, written by [[Jeremy Barlow]] under the pseudonym "Thomas Andrews" and released in [[2005]].<ref name="Empire29" /><ref>{{WebCite|url=http://jeremybarlow.com/JB/Resume.html|text=Jeremy Barlow's Resume and Work History|author=[[Jeremy Barlow]]|archivedate=20100413125020}}</ref> Her most recent appearance was in [[Star Wars: Rebellion (comic series)|''Star Wars: Rebellion''{{'s}}]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion: Small Victories|''Small Victories'']] story arc, written by Barlow and released in [[2008]].<ref name="Rebellion14" /> Dantels also received entries in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 126]]'', 2008's ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', and [[2009]]'s ''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]''.<ref name="RECG" /><ref name="FF126" /><ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|164|Dantels, Nera}}</ref> A pilot card for Dantels was included in the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game]]''{{'s}} ''[[Rebel Aces Expansion Pack]]'', first released in [[2014]].<ref name="FFGXW">{{FFGXW|set=Rebel Aces Expansion Pack|cardname=Nera Dantels}}</ref>
